core_mechanism: Collapsing the cost of a high-dimensional feasibility/enumeration problem onto a low-dimensional bottleneck by exploiting that all the nonlinear constraints factor through a low-rank (k-dimensional) map, so the complexity — and the count of connected components of the solution set — scales exponentially only in the small number k of nonlinear degrees of freedom rather than in the ambient dimension n.
brief_reason: The abstract's headline is that sampling one point per connected component of the zero set runs in (dn)^O(k) operations, polynomial for constant k and beating methods that are exponential in n, precisely because the degree-d polynomial is composed with a quadratic map Q into a k-dimensional target, so the small target dimension k rather than the domain dimension n governs the cost.
